summary: User policy for the People Bar taskbar feature. Good for a cleaner taskbar, but it removes the My People experience and related pinning UI.

setting.name: People Bar on the Taskbar
setting.casual_explanation: Windows used to offer a People Bar icon on the taskbar for quick contact access. This policy decides whether that feature is shown for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide it to reduce taskbar clutter or to remove a feature they never use.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding it gives the taskbar a cleaner look, but it also removes the My People experience and stops pinning people to the taskbar.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents HidePeopleBar through the Start Policy CSP with explicit allowed values and Group Policy mapping to HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\HidePeopleBar.

allowed_values.label: People Bar visible
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and the People Bar is not hidden.

allowed_values.label: People Bar hidden
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and the People Bar is removed from the taskbar.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes HidePeopleBar = 1 for the current user, which matches the documented hide state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1709 and later systems that still expose the People Bar policy

states.rationale: Microsoft documents the default value as 0, meaning the feature is not hidden.

windows_defaults.notes: The policy is user-scoped and defaults to not hiding the People Bar.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who still want the My People feature
avoid_for: Users who want a cleaner taskbar
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the People Bar to remain available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want this taskbar feature at all.
tradeoffs: The My People experience remains available.

recommended_profiles.label: Clean taskbar
intended_for: Users who prefer fewer taskbar items
avoid_for: Users who use My People or contact pinning

states.rationale: Hide the People Bar and remove the related UI.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the taskbar to stay simpler and more focused.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want quick contact access from the taskbar.
tradeoffs: The People icon disappears from the taskbar.
Users cannot pin people to the taskbar.

evidence.title: Microsoft Start Policy CSP: HidePeopleBar
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the scope, default value, allowed values 0 and 1, behavior, and Group Policy registry mapping for HidePeopleBar.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HidePeopleBar = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft StartMenu policy files document the exact user-policy surface, allowed values, and behavior for HidePeopleBar. The app writes the documented hide value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="HidePeopleBar" class="User" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer" valueName="HidePeopleBar">; StartMenu.adml: This policy allows you to remove the People Bar from the taskbar and disables the My People experience.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact user-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy removes the People Bar and disables the My People experience.
